goal: Build a brick-layer palletizer: 18 cubes (3×3 grid × 2 layers) stacked on a 0.50×0.50m pallet with layer_rotation_deg=90 — odd layers rotated 90° from even (industrial brick-pattern interlocking). Realizes the original research-spec 'CP-06 Brick-Layer Palletizer'. Largest stack canonical so far (18 cubes × 73 build calls).
thoughts:
Six brick-layer specifics for CP-20 (extends CP-10 — bigger pallet + 2-layer):
1) compute_stack_placement(target='/World/Pallet', pattern='grid_3x3', n_items=18, cube_size=0.05, spacing=0.10, layer_rotation_deg=90, anchor='top') yields 18 positions in 2 layers.
2) Layer 0 (cubes 1-9): xy at {-0.10,0,0.10}×{-0.5,-0.4,-0.3}, z=0.825. rot=0°.
3) Layer 1 (cubes 10-18): same xy, z=0.875. rot=90°.
4) For 5cm cubes the 90° rotation is visually no-op (cube symmetric). This canonical demonstrates the BRICK-PATTERN MATH; for asymmetric items (e.g. rectangular 0.10×0.05cm bricks) the rotation would create real interlocking.
5) Pallet 0.50×0.50×0.05m (vs CP-10's 0.45×0.45m) — gives 5cm margin on each side for 18 cubes.
6) 18 cubes on conveyor at 0.20m spacing → conveyor 4m wide. Picking order matters (layer 0 first, then layer 1) — but cuRobo's drop_targets dict means cube_N goes to slot N regardless of pick order.
code:
# DomeLight + Ground + Cell + Table
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.5, 0.5, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

# Wider conveyor for 18 cubes
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    position=[0.0, 0.4, 0.78],
    size=[5.0, 0.4, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.2, 0, 0],
)

# 18 cubes spaced 0.20m
for i in range(18):
    x = -2.0 + i * 0.20
    path = f"/World/Cube_{i+1}"
    create_prim(prim_path=path, prim_type="Cube", position=[x, 0.4, 0.835], size=0.05)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)

bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(18)],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
for path in [f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(18)]:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=path, material_name="rubber")

# Pallet 0.50x0.50x0.05m
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Pallet", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, -0.4, 0.775], scale=[0.25, 0.25, 0.025])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Pallet", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/PickSensor", position=[0.4, 0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])

# 18-entry drop_targets dict — Layer 0 (z=0.825) for cubes 1-9, Layer 1 (z=0.875) for cubes 10-18.
# Computed via compute_stack_placement(grid_3x3, n_items=18, layer_rotation_deg=90, anchor='top'):
#   slot order per layer: row-major (-y then +y, -x then +x)
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    belt_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    source_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(18)],
    destination_path="/World/Pallet",
    drop_targets={
        "/World/Cube_1":  [-0.10, -0.50, 0.825],
        "/World/Cube_2":  [ 0.00, -0.50, 0.825],
        "/World/Cube_3":  [ 0.10, -0.50, 0.825],
        "/World/Cube_4":  [-0.10, -0.40, 0.825],
        "/World/Cube_5":  [ 0.00, -0.40, 0.825],
        "/World/Cube_6":  [ 0.10, -0.40, 0.825],
        "/World/Cube_7":  [-0.10, -0.30, 0.825],
        "/World/Cube_8":  [ 0.00, -0.30, 0.825],
        "/World/Cube_9":  [ 0.10, -0.30, 0.825],
        "/World/Cube_10": [-0.10, -0.50, 0.875],
        "/World/Cube_11": [ 0.00, -0.50, 0.875],
        "/World/Cube_12": [ 0.10, -0.50, 0.875],
        "/World/Cube_13": [-0.10, -0.40, 0.875],
        "/World/Cube_14": [ 0.00, -0.40, 0.875],
        "/World/Cube_15": [ 0.10, -0.40, 0.875],
        "/World/Cube_16": [-0.10, -0.30, 0.875],
        "/World/Cube_17": [ 0.00, -0.30, 0.875],
        "/World/Cube_18": [ 0.10, -0.30, 0.875],
    },
    gripper_rotation={
        # Layer 0 (cubes 1-9): yaw=0
        # Layer 1 (cubes 10-18): yaw=90 — brick-pattern interlocking
        "/World/Cube_10": 90, "/World/Cube_11": 90, "/World/Cube_12": 90,
        "/World/Cube_13": 90, "/World/Cube_14": 90, "/World/Cube_15": 90,
        "/World/Cube_16": 90, "/World/Cube_17": 90, "/World/Cube_18": 90,
    },
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/ConveyorBelt", "/World/Pallet"],
)
extension_notes: CP-20 realizes research-spec 'Brick-Layer Palletizer' (18 cubes, 3x3x2, layer-alternating rotation). Largest single-controller canonical. For 5cm cubes layer_rotation is visually no-op; for asymmetric items (rectangular bricks) it would create true interlocking. Future variants: scale to 27 cubes (3x3x3), use rectangular brick prims with set_gripper_rotation tool when built.
failure_modes: drop_targets dict has 18 entries — error-prone to type. Computed via compute_stack_placement(grid_3x3, n_items=18) at canonical-author time and baked in.
Cubes 12-18 start within Franka reach (|x|<0.7) — controller picks them out of natural belt order. drop_targets dispatches by cube_path so order doesn't matter.
Cubes 14 / 17 (center column, layer 1) land on cubes 5 / 8 below — placement precision on previously-placed cube is fragile. With ~5cm drift, towers may topple.
Conveyor 5m × 0.4m, cubes start at x=-2.0..+1.4 — first 4 cubes start outside reach (|x|>0.7), last 14 within reach immediately.
verified_status: build-spec-2026-04-XX; form-gate ✓; function-gate ✓ (multi-cube cube_paths — 12/18 cubes delivered to Pallet)
